parties.name: JOHNSON, RAMESES 2010 JOHN MUIR PARKWAY HERCULES, CA 94547

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Relief From Order Of Involuntary Dismissal
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff RAMESES JOHNSON, in pro per present (tel: 404-542-8671).

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF RAMESES JOHNSON's Motion For Relief From Order Of Involuntary Dismissal.

Matter discussed. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Rameses Johnson's motion for relief from order of involuntary dismissal is denied. Johnson's case was dismissed as a sanction pursuant to Government Code Section 68608(b) for his failure to show cause why the matter should not be dismissed after he failed to serve the defendant, appear at case management conferences, or file written responses to the court's orders to show cause issued over a lengthy period. The complaint was filed on January 4, 2022. The case was dismissed on August 20, 2024, at which point summons had not been served. Johnson files his motion under CCP Section 473(b) and provides a declaration attesting to the reasons why he did not prosecute his case. The declaration does not specifically address the circumstances at the August 20, 2024 show cause hearing or why he missed that appearance. The August 20 order found no good cause or substantial justification to excuse Plaintiff's failure to comply with prior orders, and that a less severe sanction would be ineffective due to the history of lack of compliance. The court does not find that Plaintiff's declaration submitted with his moving papers establishes excusable neglect in light of the many opportunities that the court gave to plaintiff to comply with the court's orders to advance his case.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-20-2024 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to Government Code 68608(b). The court finds no good cause or substantial justification for failure to comply with the previous court orders. A less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Proceedings not reported. (610)
